Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix energy load sharing negative consumption when recipient has surplus (#690) (#699)

* Fix energy load sharing sending negative gain when recipient has surplus (#690)

When a recipient nation's energy_difference_variable is positive (surplus),
energy_lack was set to that positive value, making energy_load_sharing_gain_var
negative after the multiply by -1. This negative gain flowed into the
energy_dynamic_modifier's energy_gain, causing the displayed energy consumption
to go negative.

Fix by clamping energy_lack to max = 0 so only actual deficits are processed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add /fix-issue skill for finding and fixing GitHub bugs

Captures the workflow used to diagnose issue #690: listing open issues,
locating relevant code, identifying root cause, patching, validating,
and opening a pull request.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Replace UK-only channel crossings with comprehensive European ferry system (#22)

Replaces the old channelCrossings.ts (4 Dover/Eurotunnel routes) with a full
European ferry crossing system covering 88 crossings across 12 regions:
English Channel, Western Channel, North Sea, UK-Spain, UK-Ireland,
Ireland-France, Scottish Islands, Scandinavia/Baltic, Western Mediterranean,
Adriatic, Eastern Baltic, and North Africa.

New polygon-based landmass detection classifies start/end coordinates and
uses BFS pathfinding to determine mandatory crossings (different landmasses)
and optional crossings (ferry shortcuts that save driving). The Trip Wizard
now shows a Crossing step with auto-selected recommended crossings and
expandable alternatives ranked by total travel time.

Key changes:
- New src/data/ferryCrossings/ module (types, landmasses, 12 region files,
  detection engine, 48 tests)
- TripWizard.tsx rewritten crossing step with CrossingCard component,
  mandatory/optional sections, swap and toggle handlers
- TripWizardService.ts updated to use detectFerryCrossings
- Store updated: crossing → crossings array + detectedCrossings
- Deleted src/data/channelCrossings.ts

Co-authored-by: Archie Selka <Archie@Archies-MacBook-Pro-2.local>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: optimal target updates dynamically on equipment switch

Remove equipmentProfileId from optimal-comparison query key. The queryFn
doesn't use it (backend reads current session assignment), so adding it
as a 3rd key element caused a timing gap: invalidation targeted the old
key, but the component switched to a new key with no cached data, and
no refetch fired.

With the simpler key ["optimal-comparison", sessionId], the existing
invalidateQueries call in useAssignEquipment.onSuccess directly matches
the active query and triggers an in-place refetch.

Also includes grip calibration fallback for mu=1.0 equipment profiles.

Three functions in corps_front_sectors.ts used unrestricted or faction-wide
BFS, allowing brigades to be assigned to sectors across enemy territory
(e.g. Grude brigade → Bosanska Gradiska sector, 329th Mtn → Glamoč pocket).

Fixed assignOrphanedBrigadesToFaction (own-corps + friendly-territory BFS),
redistributeExcessReserves (per-corps, skip empty sectors),
ensureMinimumSectorCoverage (connectivity-checked reserve promotion).
Deleted dead bfsUnrestrictedToNearestSector(). Propagated to canon/eng docs.

commit.message:
fix: Use SameSite=None for cross-origin cookie auth

The site (mncoleman.com) and worker (mncoleman.workers.dev) are
different origins. SameSite=Strict prevented cookies from being
sent on cross-origin fetch requests, breaking authenticated API
calls. SameSite=None with HttpOnly+Secure+CORS+CSRF is the
correct pattern for cross-origin authenticated APIs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix auth on production: ProxyFix + JWT-first localhost check

Behind Railway's reverse proxy (gunicorn), request.host resolves to
localhost, causing auth middleware to skip JWT validation and set
teacher_id='local-dev' for all production users. This broke Supabase
behavior queries (invalid UUID). Fix: add ProxyFix middleware and only
fall back to 'local-dev' when no Bearer token is present.

fix(interactive): wire up progress milestones for interactive customer creation

The interactive customer creation modal was stuck at 5% because
getCustomerProgressMilestone was never provided as a dependency,
so no JOB_PROGRESS events were broadcast via WebSocket. The fallback
HTTP polling then spammed 404s since the taskId (a random UUID) has
no corresponding BullMQ job.

- Add getCustomerProgressMilestone mapping in server.ts
- Broadcast JOB_STARTED event at start of interactive save
- Reset HTTP fallback timer on JOB_STARTED in frontend

commit.message:
v1.29.9: Per-position EOD exit controls + min liquidation value gate

Move EOD closeout from a global preset setting to per-position opt-in.
New positions spawn with no EOD exit (default OFF). Users enable it
per-position via inline controls on the Signals tab.

Backend:
- Remove eod_exit_time from intraday_convexity preset (default OFF)
- Add eod_min_bid config field ($0.05 default) — skips EOD sell when
  bid is below threshold (lets worthless options expire vs paying fees)
- New execution_position_config handler for per-RM config updates
- Store eod_exit_time + eod_min_bid in _risk_config on on_start()
- Hot-modify support: update_risk_config() handles eod_min_bid
- Sync eod_min_bid to state.config alongside eod_exit_time

Frontend:
- Inline EOD controls on each active position: toggle, time, min bid
- Controls appear on both single and multi-position groups
- API route + FastAPI endpoint + ws_relay routing for position config

commit.message:
fix: scope session events by session_id to prevent stale data

getAllProjectEvents() returned events from ALL sessions without
filtering, causing old/unrelated session data to leak into compact
and resume. Replace with:

- getSessionEvents(db, sessionId) — compact uses current session only
- getLatestSessionEvents(db) — resume gets most recent session with events

Fixes across all adapters: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, VS Code Copilot.

commit.message:
fix(deploy): robust postgres health wait using docker inspect

Replace `timeout 60 bash -c 'until pg_isready...'` with a loop that polls
Docker's own healthcheck status via `docker inspect`. Benefits:
- No dependency on shell timeout command exit codes (which caused set -e to
  kill the script on first-boot when postgres takes >60s)
- Shows progress every 3s so the user can see it's working
- Extended wait to 120s for first-boot (pgvector init takes longer)
- Also waits for backend health before starting worker/frontend
- Clearer error messages pointing to docker logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix secretary cannot create patients from new appointment modal

Secretary/admin users without a doctorId caused the find_or_create_patient
RPC to receive null doctor_id, failing to resolve organization_id and
returning 400. Now validates doctor selection before opening the create
patient dialog and before saving.
